html_content:
MRI of the Lumbar Spine Without Contrast

Pathological Findings:
Disc bulging with associated annular fissuring is present at the L4–L5 level.
Normal Findings:
Alignment & Curvature
  • Lumbar alignment is preserved. No spondylolisthesis.
Vertebral Bodies
  • Vertebral body heights are preserved. No acute compression fracture.
Bone Marrow
  • Marrow signal is unremarkable for age.
Intervertebral Discs (Other Than L4–L5
  • Remaining lumbar discs are unremarkable without additional bulge, herniation, or extrusion.
Facet Joints
  • No significant facet hypertrophy or joint effusion.
Spinal Canal (Other Levels
  • No significant central canal stenosis outside the L4–L5 level.
Neural Foramina
  • No significant foraminal stenosis.
Conus & Cauda Equina
  • Conus terminates at a normal level with normal signal cauda equina roots are unremarkable without clumping.
Paraspinal Soft Tissues
  • No abnormal paraspinal edema, mass, or collection.
Impression:
Disc bulging at L4–L5 with associated annular fissuring.
Recommendations:
Clinical correlation is recommended consideration of spine specialist consultation may be warranted given the presence of bulging disc at L4–L5.
